settings.name: UI Zoom
settings.description: This changes the size of the tiles that display your metrics. The default is 100%. To fit more tiles on your screen, choose a smaller percentage. To make them larger, choose a percentage over 100%. Do not enter the percent symbol

settings.name: Heart Rate service outside FTMS
settings.description: (For Android Version 10 and above, this setting cannot be changed. This setting can be changed for Android Version 9 and below and for iOS.) When this setting is turned off, QZ sends heart rate data in a format designed to improve compatibility with third-party apps, such as Zwift and Peloton. Default is off.

settings.name: Zwift Resistance Offset
settings.description: This setting sets your “flat road” in Zwift. All communicated resistance changes will be based on this setting. The value entered is personal preference and will be dependent on your level of fitness. The suggested value for Echelon bikes is between 18 and 20. Default is 4.

settings.name: Zwift Resistance Gain
settings.description: (for bikes and treadmills when using “treadmill as a bike” setting). This setting scales the resistance from your bike or the speed from your treadmill before sending it to Zwift. Default is 1.

settings.name: Zwift Workout/Erg Mode
settings.description: Enable this setting ONLY when using Zwift in ERG (workout) Mode. QZ will communicate the target resistance (or automatically adjust your resistance if your bike has this capability) to match the target watts based on your cadence (RPM). In ERG Mode, the changes in road slope will not affect target resistance, as is the case in Simulation Mode. Default is off.

settings.name: Zwift ERG Watt Up Filter
settings.description: In ERG Mode or during a Power Zone workout on Peloton, the app sends a “target output” request. If the output requested doesn’t match your current output (calculated using cadence and resistance level), your target resistance will change to help you get closer to the target output. If the filter is set to higher values, you will get less adjustment of the target resistance and you will have to increase your cadence to match the target output. The Up and Down Watt Filter settings are the upper and lower margin before the adjustment of resistance is communicated. Example: if the up and down filters are set to 10 and the target output is 100 watts, a change of your resistance will only be communicated if your bike produces less than 90 watts or more than 110 watts. Default is 10.

settings.name: Double Negative Inclination
settings.description: Turn this on if you have a bike with inclination capabilities to fix Zwift’s bug that sends half-negative downhill inclination

settings.name: Zwift Inclination Offset
settings.description: Inclination Offset and Gain are used to adjust the incline set by Zwift instead of, or in addition to, using the QZ Zwift Gain setting. For example, when Zwift changes the incline to 1%, you can have your treadmill change to 2%. The number you enter as an offset adds to the inclination sent from Zwift or any other 3rd party app. Default is 0.

settings.name: Zwift Inclination Gain
settings.description: The number you enter as a Gain is a multiplier applied to the inclination sent from Zwift or any other 3rd party app. Default is 1.

settings.name: Resistance Offset
settings.description: Change gears using knob (Experimental)

settings.name: Speed calculates on Power
settings.description: QZ calculates speed based on your pedal cadence (RPMs). Enable this setting if you want your speed to be calculated based on your power output (watts), as Zwift and some other apps do. Default is off.

settings.name: Resistance at Startup
settings.description: (only for bikes with electronically-controlled resistance): Enter the resistance level you want QZ to set at startup. Default is 1.

settings.name: Player Age
settings.description: Enter your age so that calories burned can be more accurately calculated.

settings.name: Player Weight
settings.description: Enter your weight in kilograms so QZ can more accurately calculate calories burned. NOTE: If you choose to use miles as the unit for distance traveled, you will be asked to enter your weight in pounds (lbs) unless you enable 'Use kg for weight'.

settings.name: FTP value
settings.description: If you train to specific output (or watts) levels, for example in Peloton Power Zone classes,and have taken an FTP test (Functional Threshold Power), enter your FTP here. This number is used to calculate your Power Zones (Zones 1 to 7 for Peloton and 1 to 6 for Zwift).

settings.name: Email
settings.description: Enter your email address to receive an automated email with stats and charts when you hit STOP at the end of each workout. Make sure there are no spaces before or after the email address; this is the most common reason the automated email is not sent. Privacy Note: Email addresses are not collected by the developer and are only saved locally on your device.

settings.name: Nickname
settings.description: No need to enter data here. It is for a possible future QZ feature.

settings.name: Player Weight
settings.description: Enter your weight in kilograms so QZ can more accurately calculate calories burned. NOTE: If you choose to use miles as the unit for distance traveled, you will be asked to enter your weight in pounds (lbs) unless you enable 'Use kg for weight'.

settings.name: Pause when App Starts
settings.description: Turn on to set QZ to always open in PAUSE mode. This is important for Peloton classes so that you can sync the start of your QZ workout with the start of the Peloton class. Turn off to have QZ start tracking and timing your workout as soon as it opens.

settings.name: Treadmill Speed Forcing
settings.description: Turn this on to have QZ control the speed of your treadmill during, for example, Peloton classes based on the coach’s speed callouts. Your speed will be in the low, upper or average range based on your Peloton Options > Difficulty setting. Default is off.

settings.name: Pause when App Starts
settings.description: Turn this on to have QZ go into Pause mode upon opening when using a treadmill. This is for treadmills only. Default is off.

settings.name: Continuous Moving
settings.description: Turn this on for: - Peloton Bootcamp classes or other workouts that are on and off the bike or treadmill. QZ will continue to track your workout even when you step away from your equipment. - Capturing non-equipment-based workouts, such as yoga or strength training. NOTE: All such workouts are labeled as “Rides” in Strava, but you can edit the label in Strava.

settings.name: Cycling/Running Sensor (Peloton compatibility)
settings.description: Turn this on compatibility to Peloton over Bluetooth. Default is off.

settings.name: Run Cadence Sensor
settings.description: Forces the virtual Bluetooth bridge to send only the cadence information instead of the full FTMS metrics. Default is off.

settings.name: Bike Power Sensor
settings.description: This changes the virtual Bluetooth bridge from the standard FMTS to the Power Sensor interface. Default is off.

settings.name: Heart Belt Name
settings.description: Apple Watch users: leave it disabled! Just open the app on your watch

settings.name: Disable HRM from Machinery
settings.description: Turn this on to prevent a built-in heart rate monitor (HRM) on your exercise equipment from sending that data to QZ. This allows QZ to connect to your external HRM, such as a chest band or Apple Watch.

settings.name: Disable KCal from Machinery
settings.description: This prevents your bike or treadmill from sending its calories-burned calculation to QZ and defaults to QZ's more accurate calculation.

settings.name: Ant+ Cadence
settings.description: Turn this on if you need to use ANT+ along with Bluetooth. Power is also sent.

settings.name: Difficulty
settings.description: Typically, Peloton coaches call out a range for target incline, resistance and/or speed. Use this setting to choose the difficulty of the target QZ communicates. Difficulty level can be set to lower, upper or average. Click OK.

settings.name: Override HR Metric
settings.description: By default, QZ communicates heart rate to Peloton. Use this setting to change the metric that appears on the Peloton screen.

settings.name: Date on Strava
settings.description: Allows you to choose whether you would like the Peloton class air date to display before or after the class title on Strava.

settings.name: Activity Link in Strava
settings.description: Turn this on if you want QZ to capture a link to the Peloton class and display it in Strava.

settings.name: PZP Username
settings.description: As of 4/1/2022, this feature is broken due to a Power Zone Pack (PZP) website change. Leave (or change back to) the default of “username” (without quotation marks, all lowercase and all one word) until further notice.

settings.name: PZP Password
settings.description: As of 4/1/2022, this feature is broken due to a Power Zone Pack (PZP) website change. Leave this setting blank until further notice.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Speed in kilometers per hour. (To set your speed units to miles, go to Settings > General Options > Use Miles unit in UI).

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Bike pedal cadence in rotations per minute (RPM) or Treadmill cadence if a shoe-mounted cadence sensor or Apple Watch QZ app is used.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Estimated calories burned during session, calculated on weight, age, and watts.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Estimated distance traveled during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays your bike’s resistance. The +/- buttons can be used to change resistance, if your bike is compatible.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays the watts generated by your current effort. Watt is also referred to as output (for example, in Peloton). If your equipment does not communicate watts, QZ will calculate watts using resistance and cadence.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Estimation of weight loss during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Average watts produced for the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Percentage of current FTP and current FTP zone.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Built-in treadmill fan speed (Treadmill only)

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Cumulative power produced during the session in kilojoules.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Total time from start of the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Total time moving during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Allows you to sync resistance and cadence target changes with the Peloton coach’s callouts. If the targets are changing in QZ after the coach’s callouts, use the ‘+’ button to add seconds (essentially speeding QZ up). Use the ‘-’ button to slow QZ down. Use this tile in conjunction with the Remaining Time/Row tile (see below).

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Resistance of your bike converted to the Peloton bike scale of 1 to 100.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays target resistance in your bike’s resistance scale. For example, during a Peloton class or Zwift session, you want the resistance displayed in this tile to match the Resistance Tile.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays target resistance converted to the Peloton bike scale of 1 to 100. For example, during a Peloton class, you want the resistance displayed in this tile to match the Peloton Resistance Tile.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays target output (watts) when this information is provided by third-party apps.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays the target power zone when this information is provided by third-party apps.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: (Rower only) Displays the number of strokes rowed.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: (Rower only) Displays the stroke length.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Calculates your output (watts) divided by your weight. This is the primary metric used by Zwift and similar apps to calculate your virtual speed. NOTE: This is a much better metric to use than Output/Watts when comparing your effort to other users. This is why Peloton’s leaderboard, which uses only Output, is flawed.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Allows you to change resistance while in Auto-Follow Mode.This tile allows you override the target resistance sent by third-party apps. For example, you would use the Gears Tile to increase resistance and generate more watts for sprinting in Zwift.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays the time remaining until the next cadence and/or resistance interval.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays the next Peloton interval with duration and FTP Zone (in Power Zone classes) or Peloton Resistance (non–Power Zone classes).

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays metabolic equivalents (METs), a measurement of energy expenditure and amount of oxygen used by the body compared to the body at rest. (e.g., 4 METS requires the body to use 4 times as much oxygen than when at rest, which means it requires more energy and burns more calories).

settings.name: order index
settings.description: (Elite Rizer only) Displays steering angle.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Use this tile to display the target heart rate zone in which you’ve chosen to work out in Settings > Training Program Options.

settings.name: Zone 4 %
settings.description: Zone 5 will be calculated automatically based on Zone 4 end percentage and max HR.

settings.name: Max Heart Rate
settings.description: QZ uses a standard age-based calculation for maximum heart rate and then sets the heart rate zones based on that max heart rate. If you know your actual max heart rate (the highest your heart rate is known to reach), turn this option on and enter your actual max heart rate. Then click OK.

settings.name: Resting Heart Rate
settings.description: Enter your resting heart rate (the lowest your heart rate reaches when fully rested). This is used for accurate training load calculations. Default is 60 bpm.

settings.name: Conversion Gain
settings.description: Conversion gain is a multiplier. Use this setting to align the Peloton resistance calculated by QZ with the relative effort required by your bike. In most cases the default values will be correct.

settings.name: Conversion Offset
settings.description: Increases the resistance that QZ displays in the Peloton Resistance tile. If QZ’s calculated conversion from your bike’s resistance scale to Peloton’s seems too low, the number you enter here will be added to the calculated resistance without increasing your effort or actual resistance. (Example: If QZ displays Peloton resistance of 30 and you enter 5, QZ will display 35.)

settings.name: PID on Heart Zone
settings.description: QZ controls your treadmill or bike to keep you within a chosen Heart Rate Zone. Turn on, set a target heart rate (HR) zone in which to train and click OK. For example, enter 2 to train in HR zone 2 and the treadmill will auto adjust the speed (or resistance on a bike) to maintain your heart rate in zone 2. QZ gradually increases or decreases your speed (or bike resistance) in small increments every 40 seconds to reach and maintain your target HR zone. During a workout, you can display and use the ‘+’ and ‘-’ button on the PID HR Zone tile to change the target HR zone.

settings.name: 1 mile pace (total time)
settings.description: Enter your 1 mile time goal, click OK. This setting will be used when you’re following a training program with the speed control. These settings should also match the Zwift app settings. More info: https://github.com/cagnulein/qdomyos-zwift/issues/609.

settings.name: 5 km pace (total time)
settings.description: See 1 Mile Pace above; same except 5 km instead of 1 mile.

settings.name: 10 km pace (total time)
settings.description: See 1 Mile Pace above; same except 10 km instead of 1 mile.

settings.name: Half Marathon pace (total time)
settings.description: See 1 Mile Pace above; same except half marathon distance instead of 1 mile.

settings.name: Marathon pace (total time)
settings.description: See 1 Mile Pace above; same except marathon distance instead of 1 mile.

settings.name: Default Pace
settings.description: Select the default Pace to be used when the ZWO file does not indicate a precise pace.

settings.name: Use QT search on Android / iOS
Resistance max.
settings.description: Turn on and enter your choices for workout time (in minutes and seconds) and the maximum and minimum speed, incline (treadmill), and resistance (bike) and QZ will randomly change your speed and resistance or incline accordingly for the period of time you have selected.

settings.name: Watt Offset
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your watt output for moving your avatar faster/slower in Zwift or other similar apps as a way of calibrating your equipment. The number you enter as an Offset adds that amount to your watts.

settings.name: Watt Gain
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your watt output for moving your avatar faster/slower in Zwift or other similar apps as a way of calibrating your equipment. For example, to use a rower to cycle in Zwift, you could double your watt output to better match your cycling speed by entering 2. The number you enter is a multiplier applied to your actual watts.

settings.name: Power Averaging Mode
settings.description: If the power output/watts your equipment sends to QZ is quite variable, this setting will result in smoother Power Zone graphs. This is also helpful for use with Power Meter Pedals. Uses harmonic averaging which smooths power spikes better than arithmetic averaging. If any reading is 0, power immediately becomes 0. Default is Off. IMPORTANT NOTES: - No Average/smooth in Hometrainer config for standard home trainers which work at 1hz (No race mode available) - Disable Average on 3rd party apps (Rouvy/Zwift/MyWhoosh etc) or select 1sec in the app! - Need to use QZ in bridge mode! - For Elite home trainers or those who have a race mode (10hz), if it's not sufficient for some users, using Elite/Hometrainer smoothing in addition to QZ smoothing will improve it.

settings.name: Instant Power on Pause
settings.description: Enables the calculation of watts, even while in Pause mode. Default is off.

settings.name: Speed Offset
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your speed for moving your avatar faster/slower in Zwift if your equipment outputs speed but not watts. The number you enter as an Offset adds that amount to your speed.

settings.name: Speed Gain
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your speed output for moving your avatar faster/slower in Zwift or other apps as a way of calibrating your equipment if your equipment outputs speed but not watts. For example, to use a rower to cycle in Zwift, you could double your speed output to better match your cycling speed. The number you enter is a multiplier applied to your actual speed.

settings.name: Manual Device
settings.description: Allows you to force QZ to connect to your equipment (see “Bluetooth Troubleshooting” below). Default is “Disabled.”

settings.name: Suffix activity
settings.description: Default is “QZ.” Please leave this set to default so that other Strava users will see the QZ; a tiny bit of advertising that helps promote the app and support its development. If you choose to remove it, please consider contributing to the developer’s Patreon or Buy Me a Coffee accounts or just subscribe to the Swag bag in the left side bar to allow me to continue developing and supporting the app.

settings.name: Cadence Sensor
settings.description: Use this setting to connect QZ to your cadence sensor. Default is Disabled.

settings.name: Wheel Ratio
settings.description: Wheel ratio is the multiplier used by QZ to calculate your speed based on your cadence. For example, if you enter 1 for your wheel ratio and you are riding at a cadence of 30, QZ will display your speed as 30 km/h. The default of 0.33 is correct for most bikes.

settings.name: Session 2 HR
settings.description: Expand the bars to the right to display the options under this setting. These settings are used to calculate power (watts) for bikes that do not have power meters. Instead QZ estimates power from your cadence and heart rate. You can calibrate how QZ calculates your power from heart rate as follows: If you know that at a stable pace you produce 100W of power at a heart rate of 150 BPM and 150W at 170 BPM, you can add these values under Sessions 1 and 2 Watt and HR and QZ will calculate your power based on that trend line.

settings.name: Power Sensor
settings.description: Leave on Disabled or select from list of found Bluetooth devices.

settings.name: Power Sensor as a Bike
settings.description: If your bike doesn’t have Bluetooth, this setting allows you to use a power meter pedal sensor so your bike will work with QZ. Default is off.

settings.name: Power Sensor as a Treadmill
settings.description: If your treadmill doesn’t have Bluetooth, this setting allows you to use a Stryde sensor (or similar) so your treadmill will work with QZ. Default is off.

settings.name: Doubling Cadence for Run
settings.description: Some power sensors send cadence divided by 2. This setting will fix this behavior.

settings.name: Zwift Force Resistance
settings.description: Enables third-party apps to change the resistance of your equipment. Default is on.

settings.name: Relaxed Bluetooth for mad devices
settings.description: Leave this setting off unless the Support staff asks you to turn it on during troubleshooting. Can improve the Android Bluetooth connection to Zwift. Default is off.

settings.name: Bluetooth hangs after 30 m
settings.description: Same as “Relaxed Bluetooth for mad devices”. Leave off unless the Support staff asks you to turn it on. Default is off.

settings.name: Simulate Battery Service
settings.description: Leave this off unless the Support staff asks you to turn it on. Enables a new Bluetooth service, indicating the battery level of your device. Default is off.

settings.name: iOS Peloton Workaround
settings.description: This MUST be always ON on an iOS device. Turning it OFF will lead to unexpected crashes of QZ. Default is on.

settings.name: Android WakeLock
settings.description: Forces Android devices to remain awake while QZ is running. Default is on.

settings.name: Debug Log
settings.description: Turn this on to save a debug log to your device for use when requesting help with a bug.

settings.name: Virtual Heart Only
settings.description: Forces QZ to communicate ONLY the Heart Rate metric to third-party apps. Default is off.

settings.name: Virtual Echelon
settings.description: Enables QZ to communicate with the Echelon app. This setting can only be used with iOS running QZ and iOS running the Echelon app. Default is off.

settings.name: Virtual iFit
settings.description: Enables a virtual Bluetooth bridge to the iFit App. This setting requires that at least one device be Android. For example, this setting does NOT work with QZ on iOS and iFit to iOS, but DOES work with QZ on iOS and iFit to Android. On Android remember to rename your device into I_EL into the android settings and reboot your device.

settings.name: Virtual Rower
settings.description: Enables QZ to send a rower Bluetooth profile instead of a bike profile to third party apps that support rowing (examples: Kinomap and BitGym). This should be off for Zwift. Default is off.

settings.name: Treadmill as a Bike
settings.description: Turn on to convert your treadmill output to bike output when riding on Zwift. QZ sends your treadmill metrics to Zwift over Bluetooth so that you can participate as a bike rider. Default is off.

settings.name: Volume buttons change gears
settings.description: Allows you to change resistance during auto-follow mode using the volume buttons of the device running QZ, Bluetooth headphones or a Bluetooth remote. Changes made using these external controls will be visible in the Gears tile. This is a VERY USEFUL feature! Default is off.

settings.name: Fake Device
settings.description: Simulates QZ being connected to a bike. When this is turned on QZ will calculate KCal based on your heart rate. Examples of when to use this setting: ○ To capture Peloton class data for classes without connected equipment (e.g., a strength or yoga workout).. ○ To arrange tiles on the QZ dashboard without connecting to your equipment. ○ To use the QZ Apple Watch app without connecting to your equipment.

settings.name: Min. Resistance
settings.description: Use this setting to set a minimum target resistance. For example, if you do not want to ride at a resistance below 25, enter a value of 25 and QZ will not set a target resistance below 25. Default is 0.

settings.name: Max. Resistance
settings.description: Similar to the above, but sets a maximum target resistance. Default is 999.

settings.name: Speed Step
settings.description: (Speed Tile) This controls the amount of the increase or decrease in the speed (in kph/mph) when you press the plus or minus button in the Speed Tile. Default is 0.5 kph.

settings.name: Inclination Step
settings.description: (Incline Tile) This controls the amount of the increase or decrease in the inclination when you press the plus or minus button in the Incline Tile for both treadmills and bikes. Default is 0.5.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: (Elite Rizer only) Allows control of the incline of external inclination equipment.

settings.name: Enable Cadence color
settings.description: Bike pedal cadence changes color to indicate how your cadence compares to the cadence called out in Peloton classes. The tile displays the following colors: white if there is no target cadence in the program, red if your cadence is lower than the target, green if your cadence matches the target, and orange if your cadence is higher than the target.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays time remaining in Peloton class.

settings.name: iOS Heart Caching
settings.description: Leave this on unless you have issues connecting your Bluetooth HRM to QZ. If turning this off does not solve the connection issue, open a support ticket on GitHub. Default is on.

settings.name: Bike Weight
settings.description: Enables QZ to include the weight of your bike when calculating speed. For example, if you are competing against yourself on VZfit, adding bike weight will 'level the playing field' against your virtual self. If you have set QZ to calculate distance in miles, enter the bike weight in pounds (lbs) unless you enable 'Use kg for weight'. Default unit is kilograms (kgs).

settings.name: Cadence Offset
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your cadence output. The number you enter as an Offset adds that amount to your cadence.

settings.name: Cadence Gain
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your cadence output as a way of calibrating your equipment if your equipment outputs cadence but not watts. The number you enter is a multiplier applied to your actual cadence.

settings.name: Fake Elliptical
settings.description: Same as Fake Device but instead of simulating a bike it simulates an elliptical.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: (requires a compatible footpod with accelerometer; treadmill only) Displays stride while walking or running.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: (requires a compatible footpod with accelerometer; treadmill only) Displays time foot is on contact with ground while walking or running.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: (requires a compatible footpod with accelerometer; treadmill only) Displays the up and down movement while walking or running.

settings.name: Gender
settings.description: Select your gender so that calories burned can be more accurately calculated.

settings.name: Fake Treadmill
settings.description: Same as Fake Device but instead of simulating a bike it simulates a treadmill.

settings.name: Rolling Resistance Factor
settings.description: 0.005 = Clinchers 0.004 = Tubulars 0.012 = MTB

settings.name: MyWhoosh Compatibility
settings.description: Enables the compatibility of the Wahoo KICKR protocol to MyWhoosh app. Leave the MyWhoosh compatibility disabled in order to use Zwift.

settings.name: Wind Res. Gain
settings.description: Enable this setting ONLY when using Zwift in ERG (workout) Mode. QZ will communicate the target resistance (or automatically adjust your resistance if your bike has this capability) to match the target watts based on your cadence (RPM). In ERG Mode, the changes in road slope will not affect target resistance, as is the case in Simulation Mode. Default is off.

settings.name: Stop Treadmill at the End
settings.description: Treadmill only: enabling this if you want that QZ will stop the tape at the end of the current train program.

settings.name: Android Notification
settings.description: Android Only: enable this to force Android to don't kill QZ when it's running on background

settings.name: Strava Virtual Activity Tag
settings.description: Append the Virtual Tag to the Strava Activity

settings.name: Half Cadence on Strava
settings.description: Divide the cadence sent to Strava by 2.

settings.name: Floating Window Height
settings.description: Android Only: height of the floating window.

settings.name: Floating Window Width
settings.description: Android Only: width of the floating window.

settings.name: Floating Window % Transparency
settings.description: Android Only: transparency percentage of the floating window.

settings.name: Floating Window Startup
settings.description: Android Only: if enabled the floating window will start as soon as the fitness devices is connected.

settings.name: Restore Gears on Startup
settings.description: QZ will remember the last Gears value and it will restore on startup

settings.name: Difficulty offset based
settings.description: Target Speed and Target Incline tile offer a way to increase/decrease the current difficulty with the plus/minus buttons. By default, with this setting disabled, the speed and the inclination change with a 3% gain for every pressure. Switching this ON, QZ will add a 0.1 speed offset or a 0.5 incline offset instead.

settings.name: Resistance Smoothing
settings.description: Since this bike doesn't send resistance over Bluetooth, QZ is calculating it using cadence and wattage. The result could be a little 'jumpy' and so, with this setting, you can filter the resistance tile value. The unit is a pure resistance level, so putting 5 means that you will see a resistance changes only when the resistance is changing by 5 levels.

settings.name: Peloton Auto Sync (Experimental)
settings.description: Only for Android where QZ is running on the same Peloton device. This setting enables the AI (Artificial Intelligence) on QZ that will read the Peloton workout screen and will adjust the Peloton offset in order to stay in sync in realtime with your Peloton workout. A popup about screen recording will appear in order to notify this.

settings.name: PID on HR max
settings.description: Alternatively to 'PID on Heart Zone' setting you can use this couple of settings in order to specify a HR range.

settings.name: Strava External Browser Auth
settings.description: QZ can open an external browser to authorize Strava. Default: disabled.

settings.name: Spinups Autoresistance
settings.description: By default, QZ treats Spin-UPS in Power Zone rides as an increasing ramp to warm you up. You can disable this, to leave the resistance up to you.

settings.name: Zwift Treadmill Auto Inclination
settings.description: Only for PC where QZ is running on the same Zwift device. This setting enables the AI (Artificial Intelligence) on QZ that will read the Zwift inclination from the Zwift app and will adjust the inclination on your treadmill. A popup about screen recording will appear in order to notify this.

settings.name: Simulate Inclination with Speed
settings.description: For treadmills without inclination: turning this on and QZ will transform inclination requests into speed changes.

settings.name: Enable Companion App
settings.description: You have to install the QZ Companion App on your Garmin Watch/Computer first.

settings.name: Peloton Auto Sync Companion (Exp.)
settings.description: This setting enables the AI (Artificial Intelligence) on the QZ Companion AI app that will read the Peloton workout screen and will adjust the Peloton offset in order to stay in sync in realtime with your Peloton workout.

settings.name: Gears Gain
settings.description: Applies a multiplier to the gears. Default is 1.

settings.name: Gear Value
settings.description: Specify a particular gear value to be restored at startup. This will override the 'Restore Gears on Startup' setting.

settings.name: Rower Level
settings.description: Difficulty level for Peloton rower classes. 1 is easy 10 is hard.

settings.name: FTMS Rower
settings.description: Allows you to force QZ to connect to your FTMS Rower. If you are in doubt, leave this Disabled and send an email to the QZ support. Default is “Disabled.”

settings.name: Fake Rower
settings.description: Same as Fake Device but instead of simulating a bike it simulates a rower.

settings.name: Zwift Treadmill Auto Workout
settings.description: Only for PC where QZ is running on the same Zwift device. This setting enables the AI (Artificial Intelligence) on QZ that will read the Zwift inclination and speed from the Zwift app during a workout and will adjust the inclination and the speed on your treadmill. A popup about screen recording will appear in order to notify this.

settings.name: Pool time (ms)
settings.description: Default: 200. Change this only if you have random issues with speed or inclination (try to put 300)

settings.name: Disable wattage from machinery
settings.description: This prevents your fitness device from sending its wattage calculation to QZ and defaults to QZ’s more accurate calculation.

settings.name: FTMS Bike
settings.description: If you have a generic FTMS bike and the tiles don't appear on the main QZ screen, select here the Bluetooth name of your bike.

settings.name: FTMS Treadmill
settings.description: If you have a generic FTMS bike and the tiles doesn't appear on the main QZ screen, select here the Bluetooth name of your bike.

settings.name: ANT+ Speed Offset
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your speed sent over ANT+. The number you enter as an Offset adds that amount to your speed.

settings.name: ANT+ Speed Gain
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your speed output sent over ANT+. For example, to use a rower to cycle in Zwift, you could double your speed output to better match your cycling speed. The number you enter is a multiplier applied to your actual speed.

settings.name: Date Prefix on Strava Workout
settings.description: Append the Date to the Strava Activity as a prefix only for non-Peloton workout

settings.name: Race Mode
settings.description: By default QZ sends the info to Zwift or any other 3rd party apps with a 1000ms interval rate. Enabling the Race Mode setting will cause QZ to send them to 100ms (10hz). Of course the bottleneck will be always your bike/treadmill.

settings.name: Username
settings.description: Enter the email address you use to login to Zwift. Ensure there are no spaces before or after your email. Click OK.

settings.name: Password
settings.description: Enter the password you use to login to Zwift. Click OK. If you have entered the correct login credentials and the QZ is able to access your account, you will see a when you reopen QZ. This is a secure login, not accessible by anyone but you.

settings.name: Garmin Bluetooth Sensor
settings.description: If you want to send metrics to your Garmin device from your Mac, enable this. Otherwise leave it disabled.

settings.name: Min. Inclination
settings.description: This overrides the minimum inclination value of your treadmill (in order to reduce the inclination movement). Default is -100

settings.name: Max. Inclination
settings.description: This overrides the maximum inclination value of your treadmill (in order to reduce the inclination movement). Default is -100

settings.name: Android Force Documents/QZ Folder
settings.description: Android Only: force QZ to use the /Documents/QZ folder for debug log and fit files

settings.name: Zwift Treadmill Auto Inclination
settings.description: Only for Android and iOS: QZ will read the inclination in real time from the Zwift app and will adjust the inclination on your treadmill. It doesn't work on workout

settings.name: Zwift Click
settings.description: Use it to change the gears on QZ!

settings.name: Zwift Play
settings.description: Also for Elite Square. Use it to change the gears on QZ!

settings.name: Poll Time
settings.description: Define the number of delay seconds between each inclination change from Zwift. This value can't be less than 5. Default: 5

settings.name: Inclination Delay
settings.description: This slow down the inclination changes adding a delay between each change. This is not applied to all the model of treadmill/bike. Default is 0.

settings.name: Watt Offset
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your watt output for moving your avatar faster/slower in Zwift or other similar apps as a way of calibrating your equipment. The number you enter as an Offset adds that amount to your watts.

settings.name: Critical Power Run value
settings.description: If you train to specific output (or watts) levels, for example with Stryd,and have taken an CP test (Critical Power Test), enter your CP here. This number is used to calculate your RSS.

settings.name: Watt Offset
settings.description: You can increase/decrease your watt output for moving your avatar faster/slower in Zwift or other similar apps as a way of calibrating your equipment. The number you enter as an Offset adds that amount to your watts.

settings.name: Use speed from the power sensor
settings.description: If you have a Bluetooth treadmill and also a Stryd device connected to QZ and you want to use the speed from the stryd instead of the speed of the treadmill, enable this. Default: disabled.

settings.name: Add inclination gain factor to the power
settings.description: If you have a Bluetooth treadmill and also a Stryd device connected to QZ, by default Stryd can't get the inclination from the treadmill. Enabling this and QZ will add an inclination gain to the power read from the Stryd. Default: disabled.

settings.name: Treadmill Level
settings.description: Difficulty level for Peloton treadmill classes. 1 is easy 10 is hard.

settings.name: ID
settings.description: If you have multiple QZ instances, you can change the id of the virtual wahoo device. Default: 0

settings.name: Ant+ Bike Over Garmin Watch
settings.description: Use your garmin watch to get the ANT+ metrics from a bike

settings.name: Volume buttons debouncing
settings.description: Debounce the volume buttons, so you will only see 1 gear step if there are 2 or more volume near steps. Default is off.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: It shows 2 big gear buttons on the UI

settings.name: Swap sides
settings.description: You can swap the left to the right controller and viceversa. Default is off.

settings.name: Use Zwift app ratio for gears (Experimental)
settings.description: Use the zwift gears table instead of the QZ classic gears algorithm. Default is off.

settings.name: Gears Offset
settings.description: Applies an offset to the gears. Default is 0.

settings.name: Date Format
settings.description: Turn this on if you want QZ to capture a link to the Peloton class and display it in Strava.

settings.name: Use Resistance instead of Inclination
settings.description: For the smart trainers, use resistance instead of inclination. This should help if you don't want to have the Wahoo Climb or similar to change inclination when you change gears. Default: disabled

settings.name: Get Gears from Zwift
settings.description: This setting bring virtual gearing from zwift to all the bikes directly from the Zwift interface. You have to configure Zwift: Wahoo virtual device from QZ as for power and cadence, and your QZ device as resistance. MUST be disabled for Mywhoosh app. Default: disabled.

settings.name: PID 'Pushy'
settings.description: Enabling this the PID is trying to motivate yourself to always increase a little the effort trying anyway to keep you in the zone. Default: Enabled.

settings.name: Minimum Inclination
settings.description: If you don't want to go below a certain inclination value for bikes and treadmill set the min. value here. Default: -999.

settings.name: MQTT Host
settings.description: Enter the MQTT broker hostname or IP address

settings.name: MQTT Port
settings.description: Enter the MQTT broker port (default: 1883)

settings.name: Username
settings.description: Enter the MQTT broker username (if required)

settings.name: Password
settings.description: Enter the MQTT broker password (if required)

settings.name: Device ID
settings.description: Enter a unique device identifier for MQTT client

settings.name: Auto Start (with intro)
settings.description: Turn this on to start a workout automatically when you start a workout on Peloton (waiting the intro). Default is off.

settings.name: Auto Start (without intro)
settings.description: Turn this on to start a workout automatically when you start a workout on Peloton (skipping the intro). Default is off.

settings.name: Strava Treadmill Tag
settings.description: Append the Treadmill Tag to the Strava Activity when you are using a treadmill. If you want to see the elevation on Strava, you need to disable this.

settings.name: Treadmill Auto-adjust speed by power
settings.description: Treadmill only: Automatically adjusts speed to maintain consistent power output. Speed adjustments occur on incline changes and adapt to manual speed modifications.

settings.name: Use Garmin device in the FIT file
settings.description: With this enabled, QZ will write the FIT file as a Garmin device so Garmin will consider this fit file for the training effect. Default: disabled.

settings.name: color
settings.description: Power zone presets allow quick access to specific training zones with customizable labels and values.

settings.name: Send real inclination to virtual bridge
settings.description: By default QZ sends to the virtual Bluetooth/DIRCON bridge the current inclination of the treadmill. Enabling this, it will send instead the one wihtout considering inclination gain or offset. Default: False.

settings.name: Use inclination from the power sensor
settings.description: If you have a Bluetooth treadmill and also a Runn device connected to QZ and you want to use the inclination from the RUNN instead of the inclination of the treadmill, enable this. Default: disabled.

settings.name: PID Ignore Inclination
settings.description: Enabling this the PID will ignore the inclination changes. Default: Disabled.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heart rate Zone 1 or higher during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heart rate Zone 2 or higher during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heart rate Zone 3 or higher during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heart rate Zone 4 or higher during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heart rate Zone 5 or higher during the session.

settings.name: Align Gear Value on Both Zwift and QZ
settings.description: By default QZ is showing the actual gears from the bike. Enabling this, QZ will show the same gears that you see on Zwift. This doesn't affect the real gear value one the bike. Default: disabled.

settings.name: Disable Negative Inclination due to gear
settings.description: Enabling this QZ will ignore changing gears if the value is too low for this trainer. Default: disabled.

settings.name: Ant+ Bike
settings.description: Use this to connect to your bike using ANT+ instead of Bluetooth. Default: Disabled

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Shows Core, Body Temperature and Heat Strain Index from a Core Temperature sensor.

settings.name: iOS Bluetooth Device Native
settings.description: If you are experiencing crash on iOS midride, try to turn this on. Default is off.

settings.name: Floating Window Type
settings.description: Choose the floating window layout type. Classic uses the standard floating.htm file, while Horizontal uses the hfloating.htm file for horizontal layout.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heat Zone 1 during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heat Zone 2 during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heat Zone 3 during the session.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays total time spent in heat Zone 4 during the session.

settings.name: Rogue Echo Bike
settings.description: Enable special wattage calculation for Rogue Echo Bike: m_watt = 0.000602337 * pow(rpm, 3.11762) + 32.6404. Default is off.

settings.name: Fit File Garmin Device Training Effect Device
Show individual zone times (instead of cumulative)
settings.description: When enabled, each zone shows only the time spent in that specific zone. When disabled (default), each zone shows cumulative time spent in that zone or higher.

settings.name: ANT+ Heart Device Number (0=Auto)
settings.description: This setting enables receiving the heart rate from an external HRM over ANT+ instead of from QZ.

settings.name: Treadmill Walk Level
settings.description: Difficulty level for Peloton treadmill walking classes. 1 is easy 10 is hard.

settings.name: ERG Mode Watt Step
settings.description: Set the wattage step increment for ERG mode heart rate zone training. Default: 5 watts.

settings.name: Enable Automatic Virtual Shifting
settings.description: Enable automatic gear shifting based on cadence thresholds. When enabled, QZ will automatically shift gears up or down based on your pedaling cadence.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Button tile to switch automatic virtual shifting to Cruise profile.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Button tile to switch automatic virtual shifting to Climb profile.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Button tile to switch automatic virtual shifting to Sprint profile.

settings.name: FTMS Elliptical
settings.description: Allows you to force QZ to connect to your FTMS Elliptical. If you are in doubt, leave this Disabled and send an email to the QZ support. Default is Disabled.

settings.name: Calculate Active Calories Only
settings.description: Enable to calculate only active calories (excluding basal metabolic rate) similar to Apple Watch. When disabled, total calories including BMR are calculated. This affects both display and Apple Health integration.

settings.name: Player Height
settings.description: Enter your height for more accurate BMR and active calories calculation. Use centimeters for metric or feet'inches" format (e.g., 5'10") for imperial units.

settings.name: Calculate Calories from Heart Rate
settings.description: Enable to calculate calories based on heart rate data instead of power. Requires heart rate sensor connection for accurate calorie estimation.

settings.name: Zwift Power Offset (W)
settings.description: Add an offset in watts to the requested power from apps like Zwift. Positive values increase power, negative values decrease it. Default is 0.

settings.name: Chart Display Mode
settings.description: Choose which charts to display in the footer: both heart rate and power charts, only heart rate chart, or only power chart.

settings.name: Zwift Play Vibration
settings.description: Enable vibration feedback on Zwift Play controllers when changing gears. Default: enabled.

settings.name: Confirm Stop Workout
settings.description: Shows a confirmation popup before stopping the workout from the UI.

settings.name: Force Virtual Treadmill
settings.description: When enabled, forces QZ to impersonate a virtual treadmill regardless of the original device type. This allows any device (bike, rower, elliptical, etc.) to appear as a treadmill to third party apps. Default is off.

settings.name: Skandika X-2000 Protocol
settings.description: Enable this for Skandika X-2000 bikes. Disable for other Skandika models (e.g., HT211212095)

settings.name: Max. Speed
settings.description: This overrides the maximum speed value of your treadmill (in order to limit the max speed). Default is 100 km/h (62.1 mph)

settings.name: Rouvy Compatibility
settings.description: Wifi Compatibility for Rouvy

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Displays the total negative elevation gain (descent) in meters or feet accumulated during the workout.

settings.name: Enable Garmin Upload
settings.description: Enable automatic upload of FIT files to Garmin Connect after workouts.

settings.name: Use cadence from the power sensor
settings.description: If you have a Bluetooth treadmill and also a power sensor (like Stryd) connected to QZ and you want to use the cadence from the power sensor instead of the cadence of the treadmill, enable this. This is useful when the treadmill cadence sensor is unreliable at low speeds (walking/jogging). Default: disabled.

settings.name: Garmin device UNIT ID
settings.description: IMPORTANT: You must set your real Garmin device UNIT ID here to see your actual device in Garmin Connect. You can find your device UNIT ID in the Garmin Connect app. The default value (3313379353) is just a placeholder. If you want to see also the Acute load in Garmin Connect leave the default Unit ID here.

settings.name: Min. Speed
settings.description: This overrides the minimum speed value of your treadmill (in order to limit the min speed). Default is 0 km/h (0 mph)

settings.name: Walking Min Speed
settings.description: Minimum speed for Peloton walking sessions. Set to 0 to disable. Applied to all speed targets in walking workouts.

settings.name: Running Min Speed
settings.description: Minimum speed for Peloton running sessions. Set to 0 to disable. Applied to all speed targets in running workouts.

settings.name: Auto Lap on Segment
settings.description: Automatically trigger a lap when completing each workout segment/row. For ramp segments, lap is triggered only at the end of the ramp to avoid creating a lap every second.

settings.name: Power Averaging Mode
settings.description: If the power output/watts your equipment sends to QZ is quite variable, this setting will result in smoother Power Zone graphs. This is also helpful for use with Power Meter Pedals. Uses harmonic averaging which smooths power spikes better than arithmetic averaging. If any reading is 0, power immediately becomes 0. Default is Off. IMPORTANT NOTES: - No Average/smooth in Hometrainer config for standard home trainers which work at 1hz (No race mode available) - Disable Average on 3rd party apps (Rouvy/Zwift/MyWhoosh etc) or select 1sec in the app! - Need to use QZ in bridge mode! - For Elite home trainers or those who have a race mode (10hz), if it's not sufficient for some users, using Elite/Hometrainer smoothing in addition to QZ smoothing will improve it.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Button tile to cycle through power averaging modes: Off, 3s avg (harmonic), 5s avg (harmonic). Tap to cycle between modes. Only for bikes.

settings.name: Hardware Buttons
settings.description: Enable handling of physical Start/Pause/Stop buttons on the treadmill hardware

settings.name: Direct Distance from Treadmill
settings.description: Turn this on to read the distance directly from the treadmill instead of calculating it from speed. Some treadmills report distance more accurately than the speed-based calculation. Default is off.

settings.name: Thinkrider Controller
settings.description: Thinkrider VS200 remote controller. Use it to change gears on QZ!

settings.name: Player Weight
settings.description: Enter your weight in kilograms so QZ can more accurately calculate calories burned. NOTE: If you choose to use miles as the unit for distance traveled, you will be asked to enter your weight in pounds (lbs) unless you enable 'Use kg for weight'.

settings.name: Virtual Rower as PM5
settings.description: When enabled, the virtual rower will use the Concept2 PM5 protocol instead of FTMS. This provides compatibility with apps like Mywhoosh that only support PM5 rowers. Default is off.

settings.name: Show as %FC Max
settings.description: When enabled, displays heart rate as percentage of maximum heart rate (%FC Max) instead of BPM. AVG and MAX values will also show percentages.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Shows Heart Rate Variability (HRV) from a compatible heart rate belt. Displays RMSSD value in milliseconds.

settings.name: Step Count Gain
settings.description: Multiplier applied to the step count calculated from cadence for calibration. Increase above 1.0 to count more steps, decrease below 1.0 to count fewer steps. Default is 1.0.

settings.name: Cadence Sensor as a Treadmill
settings.description: If your equipment doesn’t have Bluetooth, these settings allow you to use a cadence sensor so it will work with QZ as a bike or treadmill. Default is off.

settings.name: Toputure TEB1
settings.description: Enable the special SPORT01 instant power formula only for the Toputure TEB1 bike. Leave disabled to use the standard FTMS instant power reported by the device.

settings.name: iOS Live Activity Right Metric
settings.description: iOS only: choose which two metrics are shown in the compact Dynamic Island bar for Live Activities. Default is Heart Rate on the left and Watt on the right.

settings.name: Gym Mode
settings.description: Useful in gyms with multiple similar machines. When enabled, QZ scans nearby equipment at startup and asks you which trainer to use before opening any Bluetooth connection.

settings.name: order index
settings.description: Current pace per mile or kilometer (Treadmill, Elliptical and Rower)

settings.name: CYCPLUS BC2 Controller
settings.description: CYCPLUS BC2 virtual shifter. Use it to change gears on QZ!

settings.name: Power Sensor Speed/Incline Coefficient A
settings.description: Power Sensor Speed/Incline Coefficient B:

settings.name: Power Sensor Speed/Incline Coefficient B
settings.description: Custom coefficients for power sensor inclination calculation using formula: vwatts = (A + B × speed) × inclination. For Stryd sensors use: A = -0.96, B = 1.33 Examples with these values: • 8 km/h, 10% incline: (-0.96 + 1.33×8) × 10 = 97W added • 11 km/h, 10% incline: (-0.96 + 1.33×11) × 10 = 137W added If both A and B are 0, QZ will use the default formula: 9.8 × weight × (inclination/100). Default: A = -0.96, B = 1.33

settings.name: Custom CSC Resistance/Watt Table
settings.description: Enable a custom linear resistance/watt table for CSC bikes. Joroto bikes keep using their dedicated resistance power profile. Resistance is clamped using the existing Min. Resistance and Max. Resistance settings.

settings.name: Watt 2
settings.description: QZ will build a linear equation from the two resistance/watt points and clamp the effective resistance using the existing Min. Resistance and Max. Resistance settings.

settings.name: Use Apple Watch Cadence for Fake Treadmill Speed
settings.description: iOS only. For Fake Treadmill mode: when no physical treadmill is connected, derives Speed from Apple Watch step cadence using the Wheel Ratio under Accessories > Cadence Sensor Options. The cycling default is far too high for running - try 0.04-0.15 depending on pace, from walking to running, and tune to taste. Useful with apps like Kinomap or Zwift. Default is off.

settings.name: Enable Keyboard Shortcuts
settings.description: Click on a field and press a key to assign a shortcut. Press Backspace to clear. Preset fields follow the same left-to-right order as the preset buttons.
